name: The Sacrifice
description: A protagonist relinquishes something of great value for a greater good, affirming moral commitment.

nodes.label: Valued Life
nodes.definition: The protagonist has something genuinely worth living for — love, purpose, community, or personal fulfillment. The audience must feel what will be lost.
entry_conditions: Audience sees what the character treasures
exit_conditions: A threat emerges that can only be stopped at enormous personal cost
typical_variants: Family life that brings joy
A mission or calling the character loves
Relationships that define the character's identity
A future the character has worked toward
failure_modes: The character has nothing to lose — sacrifice carries no weight
The valued life is shown too briefly to register
signals_in_text: The character expressing what matters to them

nodes.label: Threat Emerges
nodes.definition: A danger arises that threatens something larger than the protagonist — a community, a cause, or people the character loves. The threat cannot be stopped by ordinary means.
exit_conditions: The protagonist understands the scale of the threat and that it demands extraordinary response
typical_variants: Invasion or catastrophe threatening the community
A loved one in mortal danger
A cause about to be lost without decisive action
A systemic evil that can only be stopped at personal cost
failure_modes: Threat is too abstract to feel real
Threat could be stopped by easier means, making sacrifice unnecessary

nodes.label: Realization of Cost
nodes.definition: The protagonist understands that stopping the threat requires giving up something irreplaceable — their life, their freedom, their relationships, or their future.
entry_conditions: All lesser options have been exhausted or are clearly insufficient
exit_conditions: The protagonist fully understands the price and has not yet decided to pay it
typical_variants: Discovering that only their death will stop the threat
Realizing that saving others means losing their own future
Understanding that the sacrifice will go unrecognized
Learning that the cost includes harm to those they love
failure_modes: Realization is too abrupt — no time to process
The cost is unclear or seems manageable, undermining the sacrifice's weight
signals_in_text: Calculation of what must be given up
The character looking at what they will lose

nodes.label: Resistance and Struggle
nodes.definition: The protagonist struggles with the decision. They seek alternatives, resist the necessity, or are tempted to let someone else pay the cost. This struggle makes the eventual choice meaningful.
entry_conditions: The character has not yet committed
exit_conditions: All alternatives are exhausted; the character must choose
typical_variants: Frantically searching for another way
Others volunteering but being refused
Temptation to walk away and let the threat proceed
Bargaining with fate or with the antagonist
failure_modes: Character accepts the sacrifice instantly — no struggle diminishes the choice
Struggle goes on so long the urgency is lost

nodes.label: The Decision
nodes.definition: The protagonist chooses to make the sacrifice. This is a deliberate, conscious act — not an accident or an impulse. The character knows what they are giving up and chooses to give it.
entry_conditions: The threat remains and demands action
exit_conditions: The protagonist commits to the sacrificial act
typical_variants: Quiet resolve after a final goodbye
Public declaration in front of those being saved
Private decision made in solitude
A decision made in the heat of the moment but with full awareness
failure_modes: Decision is made by accident — removes moral agency
Decision is forced by others — reduces the character's choice
No visible moment of decision — the sacrifice just happens
signals_in_text: Letting go of the thing they wanted most

nodes.label: The Sacrificial Act
nodes.definition: The protagonist executes the sacrifice. The cost is paid — visibly, irrevocably, and with full awareness. This is the story's climactic moment.
exit_conditions: The sacrifice is complete; the cost is paid
typical_variants: Physical death to stop the threat
Permanent separation from loved ones
Giving up power, freedom, or identity
Taking on suffering that others would have borne
failure_modes: Sacrifice is undermined by a last-minute rescue
The cost turns out to be less than advertised
Sacrifice is graphically depicted but emotionally empty

nodes.label: World Saved
nodes.definition: The threat is stopped and those who were saved must live with the cost that was paid. The sacrifice's meaning is reflected in how the world responds.
exit_conditions: A new equilibrium is established — marked by the absence of the protagonist or what they gave up
typical_variants: Mourning and honor for the fallen
The saved community living in the peace that was bought
Quiet recognition by those who knew the cost
The sacrifice becomes legend or is forgotten — both carry meaning
failure_modes: The world doesn't seem to register the sacrifice
The cost is immediately undone (resurrection, return)
The aftermath is so brief the sacrifice has no resonance
signals_in_text: Others carrying on with awareness of the cost
Changed behavior in those who were saved

edges.label: Danger Arrives
edges.preconditions: Valued life is established; an existential danger appears
edges.effects_on_stakes: Everything the character values is now at risk
edges.effects_on_character: The protagonist shifts from living to defending what they live for
edges.common_alternatives: Threat builds gradually; threat strikes suddenly
edges.anti_patterns: Threat is too small to require sacrifice

edges.label: Cost Becomes Clear
edges.preconditions: The threat is known; conventional solutions have failed or are clearly insufficient
edges.effects_on_stakes: The stakes become personal and specific — the protagonist's own life or future
edges.effects_on_character: Horror and grief as the character understands what is required
edges.common_alternatives: Cost is discovered through investigation; cost is delivered as an ultimatum
edges.anti_patterns: Cost seems avoidable — the sacrifice will feel unnecessary

edges.label: Fights the Necessity
edges.preconditions: The cost is understood; the character does not want to pay it
edges.effects_on_stakes: Each failed alternative makes the sacrifice more certain
edges.effects_on_character: Fear, grief, and anger battle with duty and love
edges.common_alternatives: Resistance is frantic searching; resistance is quiet denial; resistance involves others trying to take the protagonist's place
edges.anti_patterns: Character accepts instantly with no struggle — cheapens the sacrifice

edges.label: Chooses to Pay the Price
edges.preconditions: All alternatives are exhausted; the character faces the choice directly
edges.effects_on_stakes: The protagonist's fate is now sealed by their own choice
edges.effects_on_character: A moment of transcendence — fear gives way to purpose
edges.common_alternatives: Decision is calm; decision is anguished; decision surprises even the character
edges.anti_patterns: Decision is made for the character by circumstance — removes moral agency

edges.label: Executes the Sacrifice
edges.preconditions: The decision is made; the character acts
edges.effects_on_character: The character becomes what they chose to be in the defining moment
edges.common_alternatives: Act is public and witnessed; act is private and discovered later
edges.anti_patterns: The sacrifice is interrupted and made unnecessary at the last second

edges.label: The Cost Is Felt
edges.preconditions: The sacrifice is complete; the threat is resolved
edges.effects_on_stakes: External danger is over; the absence of what was sacrificed defines the new world
edges.effects_on_character: The protagonist's choice ripples outward through those who remain
edges.common_alternatives: Aftermath is celebratory with an undertone of grief; aftermath is primarily grief with gratitude emerging later
edges.anti_patterns: The cost is immediately reversed or forgotten
